channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Albanese's Singapore Trip: Navigating Australia's Fuel Security Amid Global Tensions
item.description: Australia's Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is set to make a swift visit to Singapore, the country's largest fuel supplier, to address rising petrol prices. The move comes at a time of global geopolit...

item.title: Mumbai begins construction of India’s first autonomous AI pod taxi system
item.description: Mumbai has initiated the groundwork for India’s pioneering AI-driven pod taxi service, marking a significant step towards modernising urban transportation in the country. The project aims to introduce...

item.title: Cabinet Approves Revised White Paper on Citizenship and Immigration
item.description: South Africa’s Minister of Home Affairs, Dr Leon Schreiber, has praised Cabinet's approval of the Revised White Paper on Citizenship, Immigration, and Refugee Protection, a significant policy update f...

item.title: Atlassian introduces new visual AI and integrated agent features for Confluence
item.description: On April 8, 2026, Atlassian unveiled new artificial intelligence tools and agents for its collaboration platform Confluence, aiming to make data visualization and AI integration more accessible within...

item.title: Impact of Rating Agency Mistakes on Africa’s Economies
item.description: The S&P Global Africa Credit Rating Trends 2025 report has significant flaws that could affect how investors view African economies. Key mistakes include mislabelling Burundi as Uganda, merging Sudan ...

item.title: Oil Prices Fall Sharply Amid Global Demand Concerns and Market Adjustments
item.description: Global oil prices fell sharply on April 8, 2026, marking what analysts say could be the steepest decline since the early months of the Covid-19 pandemic. Brent crude and West Texas Intermediate both d...

item.title: NNPC Begins Export of Cawthorne Crude to Netherlands to Boost Production
item.description: NNPC Limited don start to dey export one new crude oil wey dem call 'Cawthorne blend' go Netherlands. Dis new crude na light and sweet oil with API gravity of 36.4, wey fit compete with Bonny Light fo...

item.title: Alex Eala exits Linz Open after loss to Jelena Ostapenko
item.description: Filipina tennis star Alex Eala suffered a tough defeat at the Linz Open after falling to Latvian Grand Slam champion Jeļena Ostapenko. Eala, who continues to compete on the professional circuit follow...

item.title: Kenya Responds to UN Report on Haiti Mission, Denies Rape Allegations
item.description: Kenya's Prime Cabinet Secretary, Musalia Mudavadi, has formally addressed a United Nations report claiming Kenyan officers in Haiti were involved in sexual exploitation and abuse. Mudavadi's letter to...

item.title: Australia Faces Diesel Shortages and Price Volatility Due to Global Events
item.description: Australia is currently experiencing significant disruptions to its fuel supply chain, particularly impacting diesel availability in regional areas. The crisis stems from ongoing geopolitical tensions,...

item.title: Assam 2026 Elections: Key Contests, Identity Politics and Party Dynamics
item.description: As Assam prepares to go to polls on April 9, 2026, Assamese nationalism, locally called 'Asomiya Jatiyotabad', remains a defining issue. The elections are not merely about forming a new government but...

item.title: Addressing the Rise of Antisemitism in Canada
item.description: In Canada, a country built on diversity and the principles of equality, the recent surge in antisemitism is alarming. Jon Love, in his article for the National Post, highlights that Jewish Canadians a...

item.title: Bluesfest cancellation reveals financial risks and instability in Australia’s live music industry
item.description: The sudden collapse of the Byron Bay Bluesfest has shone a harsh light on the vulnerabilities of Australia’s live music sector. Festival organiser Peter Noble, following the aggressive contract model ...

item.title: Saudi Arabia Expands Saudisation, Restricts Foreign Workers in 69 Administrative Roles
item.description: Saudi Arabia has expanded its Saudisation programme, now covering 69 administrative support professions in the private sector. Effective from April 5, 2026, certain roles such as secretaries, translat...

item.title: Constitutional Court to Deliver Phala Phala Judgment Within a Month, Says Chief Justice
item.description: Chief Justice Mandisa Maya has confirmed to EFF leader Julius Malema that the Constitutional Court will deliver its judgment on the Phala Phala case within a month. This comes after the EFF raised con...

item.title: New Brunswick to Implement Toll Despite Economic Concerns from Chamber of Commerce
item.description: New Brunswick's Premier, Blaine Higgs, has confirmed that a new toll will be introduced at the province's boundary with Nova Scotia, despite concerns raised by the local Chamber of Commerce. The toll ...

item.title: Higher Heat Index Expected Across More Areas as PH Enters Peak of Dry Season
item.description: The Philippines is now entering the peak of its warm and dry season, and with it, many areas are experiencing a rise in the heat index. The heat index, which measures how hot it feels when humidity is...

item.title: Manitoba community calls for improved mental health support after youth charged in school threat
item.description: In Rivers, Manitoba, parents have expressed serious concerns about a 14-year-old student charged in connection with a planned school attack. They allege the youth had previously threatened several chi...

item.title: Mwinyi Honors Karume's Legacy in Zanzibar's Revolutionary History
